title: Sam Witwer
content:

|image=[[File:SamWitwer.jpg]]

|birth=[[October 20]], [[1977]]<ref name="This Week Hype">{{ThisWeek|WDz6G4BMe7c|The Mandalorian Season Two Hype, Tales from the Galaxy's Edge Action, and More!}}</ref>
|death=

'''Samuel Stewart "Sam" Witwer''' (born [[October 20]], [[1977]]<ref name="This Week Hype" />) is an American actor.

==Biography==
When [[Amy Beth Christenson]]'s original concept art of [[Galen Marek]] looked strikingly like Sam Witwer, the vice president at the time noted the resemblance due to recognizing Witwer from ''Battlestar Galactica'', so he was contacted to audition.<ref name="SWU Interview">{{WebCite|url=http://www.theforce.net/videogames/story/swu_interviews_sam_witwer_123563.asp|text=SWU Interviews Sam Witwer|date=2009-06-15|archivedate=20160102042927}}</ref>

Witwer was a friend of [[LucasArts]] sound director [[David W. Collins]] and wished to compete for the role. His audition included a scene where Marek/The Apprentice was assembling a [[lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] through [[telekinesis/Legends|telekinesis]] in a [[meditation chamber/Legends|meditation chamber]]. Witwer employed his knowledge of ''Star Wars'' lore and Marek's backstory to portray the scene, as a character whose real challenge was not fighting, but finding peace in himself to concentrate, and contorted his features accordingly, as if struggling to remember something. Witwer filled the role within the initial auditions.<ref>''[[The Art and Making of Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]''</ref>

From there, he became the complete basis for the main character's voice and appearance in ''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]''. His likeness was created through motion capture for the game to build the character's appearance and range of facial expressions. Witwer's performance was recorded on HD cameras (as were all the actors who worked on the game). While the game was in production, Witwer only knew his character as "Starkiller."<ref name="SWU Interview" /> Witwer also voiced Galen Marek (known only as "The Apprentice") in ''[[Soulcalibur IV]]''. Witwer returned to play [[Subject 1138|Starkiller]] in the sequel ''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II]]''. For this character, he served in the same capacity as before by providing his voice and likeness to the main character.<ref name="Force Unleashed II">''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II]]''</ref> Witwer also voiced [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] in ''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed'',<ref name="Force Unleashed">''[[Star Wars: The Force Unleashed]]''</ref> and gave his voice and likeness to the [[Aberrant clone|imperfect genetic replicas]] and the [[Dark Apprentice]] in ''Star Wars: The Force Unleashed II''.<ref name="Force Unleashed II" />

Afterwards, Witwer appeared on the ''[[Star Wars: The Clone Wars]]'' television series as the voices of the [[Son]]<ref name="TCW Overlords">{{TCW|Overlords}}</ref> and [[Maul]].<ref name="TCW Brothers">{{TCW|Brothers (episode)}}</ref>

Witwer took over voicing [[Darth Sidious|Palpatine]] from [[Tim Curry]] for the second season of ''[[Star Wars Rebels]]'' along with voicing [[W1-LE]] and an Imperial [[Technician]].<ref name="Rebels Siege of Lothal">{{Rebels|Star Wars Rebels: The Siege of Lothal}}</ref>

Witwer voiced various characters in {{Film|VII}}, including several [[First Order stormtrooper|stormtroopers]], an announcer and a [[First Order]] [[officer]].<ref name="awakensvoices">{{SW|url=news/from-blast-that-x-wing-to-traitor-the-voices-of-star-wars-the-force-awakens|text=From "Blast that X-Wing!" to "Traitor!": The voices of ''Star Wars: The Force Awakens''}}</ref>

Witwer expressed an interest in being in the [[Star Wars: Underworld|live-action TV series]], presumably reprising his role as Galen Marek.<ref name="UpClose">{{WebCite|url=http://www.wizarduniverse.com/magazine/toyfare/006564394.cfm|text=Up Close: Sam Witwer|author=Livingston, Andy|date=2007-12-04|archivedate=20071207171048}}</ref>

On [[May 27]], [[2020]], it was announced on [[StarWars.com]] and ''[[The Star Wars Show]]'' that Witwer would be portraying the voice of the [[Dark side of the Force|dark side]] in the web game series ''[[Star Wars: Jedi Temple Challenge]]''.<ref name="StarWars.com">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-jedi-temple-challenge-premiere|text=''Star Wars: Jedi Temple Challenge'' Challenge to Debut June 3 on ''Star Wars'' Kids}}</ref><ref name="Collider Star Wars">{{WebCite|url=https://collider.com/star-wars-jedi-temple-challenge-trailer-youtube|text=The Force Is Strong With This Epic 'Star Wars: Jedi Temple Challenge' Trailer|author=Allie Gemmill|date=2020-05-27|archivedate=20200527225642}}</ref>
